source: NvChad - Using vale in NvChad: what vale is, the nvim-lint plugin, and installing vale with Mason

options: An open-source prose/style linter for technical writers
questions.why: vale checks writing voice and style consistency.

questions.q: Which plugin adds linter support to NvChad (vale rides on it)?
questions.why: nvim-lint integrates linters like markdownlint, vale and yamllint.

questions.q: To install nvim-lint you create which file?
questions.why: Create lua/plugins/nvim-lint.lua; NvChad picks it up on restart.

options: a filetype (e.g. markdown) to its list of linters

questions.q: vale itself is installed through which tool inside nvim?
questions.why: Mason installs vale and keeps it up to date.

questions.q: In the Mason list, pressing menu item 4 does what?
questions.why: Item 4 filters the list to linters so you can find vale.

questions.q: With the cursor on vale in the Mason list, which key installs it?
questions.why: Press i to install the package under the cursor.
